examples.title: Node Structure Definition and Memory Allocation
examples.description: Defines the node structure for singly linked list and allocates memory for a new node
examples.explanation: Defines a self-referential structure 'node' containing integer data and pointer to next node. The createNode function allocates memory using malloc, assigns data, sets next to NULL, and returns the new node.

examples.title: Insert Node at Beginning
examples.description: Inserts a new node at the beginning of the singly linked list
examples.explanation: Takes pointer to head pointer (double pointer). Creates new node, makes its next point to current head, then updates head to point to new node. This ensures the new node becomes the first node.

examples.title: Delete First Node
examples.description: Deletes and frees the first node of the singly linked list
examples.explanation: Checks if list is empty first. Stores current head in temporary pointer, moves head to point to next node, then frees the memory of original first node. Setting temp to NULL prevents dangling pointer.

examples.title: Traverse and Display List
examples.description: Traverses the entire linked list and prints all node values
examples.explanation: Uses while loop starting from head, prints data, moves to next node using current->next. Loop continues until current becomes NULL (end of list). Handles empty list as special case.

examples.title: Delete Node with Specific Value
examples.description: Deletes the first occurrence of a node containing given value
examples.explanation: Uses two pointers: prev and current. First checks if the key is at head. Then traverses until key is found or list ends. If found, updates prev->next to skip current node, then frees current. Handles edge cases of key at head and key not found.

examples.title: Singly Linked List in Python
examples.description: Implementation of singly linked list with basic operations in Python
examples.explanation: Python class-based implementation where Node is a class with data and next attributes. LinkedList class manages head pointer and operations. Python's garbage collector handles memory deallocation automatically.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for insertion, O(n) for traversal
